'''Ben Kusto''' (born 23 February 1976) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s.

==Playing career==
Kusto started his career off with St. George where he played for 3 seasons before moving to Parramatta.  Kusto played in St. George's final game before they formed a joint venture with the [[Illawarra Steelers]] to become [[St. George Illawarra]].  A semi-final loss to [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]] at [[Kogarah Oval]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-1998/minor-prelim-semi/st-george-vs-canterbury/summary.html/|title=St George Dragons 12 lost to Canterbury Bankstown Bulldogs 20|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=9 January 2020|archive-date=14 September 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190914041946/http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-1998/minor-prelim-semi/st-george-vs-canterbury/summary.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/08/27/disgruntled-st-george-illawarra-dragons-fans-the-games-biggest-crisis-merchants/|title=Disgruntled Dragons fans the game's biggest 'crisis merchants'|website=www.nrl.com}}</ref>  

Kusto spent two seasons at Parramatta and was part of the 2000 Parramatta side which the media dubbed "The Baby Eels".  Kusto left Parramatta at the end of the [[2000 NRL season]] to join English side Huddersfield.  

Kusto was part of the Huddersfield side which were relegated from the Super League.  Kusto then signed a contract to rejoin Parramatta.  At the end of the [[2002 NRL season]], Kusto was released by the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/articles/2002/08/05/1028157909316.html|title=Kusto emerges from the wilderness ready to skin Rabbits - smh.com.au|website=www.smh.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/eels/how-jarryd-haynes-gold-coast-titans-signing-allowed-mitch-moses-to-arrive-early-at-parramatta-eels/news-story/10d89433924f6818b608567534ad35ef|title=Jarryd Hayne's Parramatta Eels snub: Mitchell Moses buy thanks to Gold Coast Titans move|date=17 August 2017|publisher=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2010/07/07/rivalry-round-clashes-panthers-v-eels/|title=Rivalry Round Clashes: Panthers v Eels|date=7 July 2010|publisher=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/about/official-player-numbers/|title=Official Player Numbers|website=Parramatta Eels}}</ref>
